Supersonic jets could revolutionary coast-to-coast travel in the US.

Regulatory streamlining for supersonic aircraft could unlock a $100+ billion market opportunity as manufacturers compete to break the subsonic-speed ceiling constraining US domestic routes.

Safety Mar 26, 2026

A Hot Start to Spring in the Southwest

Temperatures in several states climbed over 100 degrees Fahrenheit in March 2026, which in some places was the highest March temperature on record.

Record March heat in the Southwest may increase aircraft density altitude challenges and reduce takeoff performance for regional operators throughout the busy spring season.

Safety Mar 26, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS frameworks shift aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before they cause incidents.

The survivors of AC8646 remain empty-handed.

Aircraft recovery logistics directly impact passenger claim resolution timelines, highlighting the need for carriers to establish expedited salvage protocols for belongings retrieval.

Drones Mar 26, 2026

DJI just nuked drone prices across the board

Something strange is happening to DJI drone prices right now. And if you’ve ever even briefly considered buying a drone, this is one of those moments where ignoring it might come back to haunt you. Because this isn’t a typical seasonal sale or a small round of discounts tucked away on a product...

DJI's aggressive pricing strategy may force competitors to restructure their product-market positioning, potentially accelerating consolidation in the commercial and consumer drone sectors.

See DroneDJ →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Why The World’s Most-Produced Fighter Jet May Finally Disappear In 2026

Why the last combat-worthy MiG-21s likely disappeared in 2025, with only on-paper rusting hulks in service in 2026.

The MiG-21's retirement marks the end of a 60+ year production run, eliminating a crucial low-cost fighter option for developing nations and reshaping global air force modernization timelines.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Organizations that psychologically empower frontline employees to report hazards without fear of retaliation achieve measurably better safety outcomes than those relying solely on compliance procedures.

La NASA ofrecerá cobertura en vivo de los eventos previos al lanzamiento, el...

NASA's comprehensive media coverage strategy for Artemis II demonstrates the agency's commitment to public engagement and transparency throughout the lunar mission's critical phases.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Ames Experts Available for Artemis II Flight Test Interviews

NASA’s Ames Research Center in Silicon Valley invites media to interview local subject matter experts on Friday, March 27 from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. ahead of the agency sending astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years with the Artemis II flight test. NASA teams are gearing up...

NASA Ames is positioning its Silicon Valley expertise as a key technical resource for media coverage of the first crewed lunar mission since Apollo, signaling institutional emphasis on public communication around Artemis II.

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit design at major hubs can create confusion for pilots during post-landing procedures, requiring heightened crew situational awareness.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Sets Coverage for First Artemis Crewed Mission Around Moon

A variety of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for NASA’s Artemis II mission around the Moon will stream online. The agency is targeting no earlier than Wednesday, April 1, for the test flight during a two-hour window that opens at 6:24 p.m. EDT, with additional launch opportunities through...

NASA's April 2025 Artemis II launch window signals the agency's accelerated timeline for returning humans to lunar orbit after a multi-year delay, with extensive public streaming enabling real-time mission transparency.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Space Force weighs launch alternatives as Vulcan faces potential months-long grounding

Officials explore shifting missions, extending satellites already in orbit

Space Force's contingency planning signals industry-wide supply chain vulnerability, forcing aerospace contractors to accelerate development of alternative launch systems to reduce dependency on single providers.

Safety Mar 26, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

Reno-Tahoe's Mustang 9 Departure requires heightened pilot attention due to specific procedural demands on runways 17L/R that differ from standard departure operations.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Delta Air Lines Continues Los Angeles Lead, Schedules More Flights To Florida This Winter

Delta is adding more Florida flying from LAX as its battle with United intensifies.

Delta's Florida route expansion from LAX signals a strategic shift to capture leisure travel demand during peak winter season, directly challenging United's market position on high-yield transcontinental routes.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

JetBlue Considers Selling Itself To A Major US Carrier, Weighs How Washington Will React

JetBlue's exploration of a potential sale to rival airlines has sparked intense speculation and antitrust scrutiny.

JetBlue's sale consideration signals weakening competitive viability, but regulatory approval from DOJ remains the primary hurdle given recent antitrust enforcement trends.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Up To 22-Hour Flights: United Airlines' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Discover the airline's most extended nonstop and one-stop services, some of which are quite controversial.

Ultra-long flights demand operational innovation in crew scheduling, aircraft maintenance windows, and passenger comfort that may reshape regional hub economics.

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Airlines can simultaneously increase capacity and reduce emissions by optimizing ground operations, air traffic management, and flight routing rather than building new infrastructure.

See MDPI →

This article will provide the reader with an in-depth look at the US airlines with the most spacious Premium Economy Seats In 2026.

Premium economy seat width competition among US carriers signals a strategic shift toward capturing mid-tier passengers willing to pay above economy fares but below business class.

NASA Sets Coverage for Artemis II Moon Mission

Editor’s note: NASA will continuously update this Artemis II briefings and mission events page throughout prelaunch, launch, and mission activities. NASA will provide live coverage of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for the agency’s upcoming Artemis II crewed test flight around the Moon....

NASA's comprehensive media strategy for Artemis II demonstrates the agency's commitment to public transparency during critical human spaceflight operations that will validate lunar return capabilities.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Artemis Moon Tree Dedicated in Honor of Mary W. Jackson

On March 18, 2026, students, staff, and members of NASA’s Langley Research Center gathered at Mary W. Jackson Elementary School in Hampton to celebrate the dedication of a remarkable addition to the campus – an Artemis Moon Tree. Although formally dedicated on this day, the loblolly pine had...

NASA's symbolic planting of moon-traveled tree seeds honors Jackson's legacy while inspiring the next generation of aerospace engineers and scientists at educational institutions.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

US House passes bill to clear way for overland supersonic flights

The US House of Representatives passed legislation aimed at opening the skies to civil supersonic flight over land,…

Lifting the overland supersonic ban removes a decades-old regulatory barrier that could unlock a new market for manufacturers like Boom and Spike, contingent on noise certification standards.

Eve flies eVTOL prototype for Brazilian president in high-profile test milestone

Eve Air Mobility flew its full-scale engineering prototype for Brazilian authorities, including President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva,…

Eve's demonstration to Brazilian leadership signals government backing that could accelerate eVTOL certification and infrastructure investment in Latin America.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Names Jennifer Lyons Acting Launch Services Program Manager

NASA has selected Jennifer Lyons as acting program manager for the agency’s Launch Services Program (LSP) based at Kennedy Space Center in Florida. In this role, Lyons will lead NASA’s acquisition and management of domestic commercial launch services for science and robotic exploration missions...

NASA's LSP leadership transition signals continued reliance on commercial launch providers for science missions, with Lyons overseeing critical vendor relationships and contract management during a period of increased launch cadence.

See NASA →

BRINC Unveils Guardian, Launching the Next Era of Drone as First Responder

Guardian delivers 24/7 operations, unmatched imaging, Starlink connectivity, and more to help public safety agencies

Guardian's Starlink integration enables first responders to operate drones beyond traditional communication constraints, addressing critical connectivity gaps in remote or disaster-affected areas.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Office of Space Commerce releases mission authorization proposal

The Office of Space Commerce has rolled out its proposal for a “light touch” approach to mission authorization, the latest attempt in an effort to regulate new commercial space applications.

The OSC's light-touch authorization framework aims to reduce regulatory barriers for commercial space ventures while maintaining safety oversight, potentially accelerating market entry for emerging aerospace technologies.

See SpaceNews →

Textron Aviation To Showcase Full Range Of Aircraft At Sun ’n Fun

Textron Aviation will display Cessna, Beechcraft, and Pipistrel aircraft together for the first time at Sun ’n Fun 2026.

Textron's unified product display signals a strategic pivot toward integrated marketing of its diverse aircraft portfolio to reach broader customer segments.

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

Cirrus posts strong 2025 with 691 SR Series deliveries, 106 Vision Jets

Cirrus Aircraft reported it delivered 691 SR Series piston aircraft and a record 106 Vision Jets in 2025,…

Cirrus's Vision Jet momentum signals accelerating market acceptance of single-engine jets for personal aviation, with record turbine deliveries outpacing traditional piston aircraft growth rates.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Webb Captures Saturn in Infrared

Saturn’s icy rings glow in this infrared view from NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope released on March 25, 2026. Combining this image with another that NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope captured – also released on the same day – provides scientists with a richer, more layered understanding of the gas...

Webb's infrared Saturn observations complement Hubble's visible-light data, enabling multi-spectrum analysis that enhances atmospheric and ring composition understanding for planetary science.

See NASA →

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

Study Unveils Redevelopment Concepts For Burke Lakefront Airport

A new economic study outlines two potential mixed-use redevelopment plans for Burke Lakefront Airport, part of Mayor Justin Bibb’s effort to close the city-operated airport.

Burke Lakefront Airport's closure could repurpose valuable lakefront real estate into mixed-use development, signaling a broader trend of municipalities converting underutilized urban airports into commercial and residential assets.
